Okay, it sounds like you're going through a really difficult time. It takes a lot of courage to acknowledge feeling stuck and exhausted. It’s understandable that you’d feel that way when things feel like they’re repeating and draining you. That thought, "I wonder if I even deserve to feel better," is particularly striking – it suggests a deep sense of self-doubt and perhaps a belief that you're not worthy of relief. 

Can you tell me a little more about what that thought – "I wonder if I even deserve to feel better" – feels like when it comes up? What’s happening around you or inside you that makes you think that?

patient.query: It feels heavy, like a weight I can’t shake off. When I think that way, it’s usually when I’m alone, maybe after a day where I just feel like I didn’t accomplish anything or when I let myself down in some way. It’s like this little voice tells me that I should just accept things as they are because maybe I’m not trying hard enough. I don’t know... it just makes me feel more isolated, like I’m not allowed to hope for something better

triggers: after a day of feeling unaccomplished
automatic_thoughts: I should just accept things as they are
maybe I’m not trying hard enough

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

patient.query: It just says things like, “You’re not good enough” or “You’ll never change.” It feels like it's pointing out my failures, like when I mess up or don’t get things right. I guess I believe it because it just feels… true, you know? Sometimes, when I look back at my life, all I see are the moments where I fell short. It’s hard to argue with something that feels so familiar.

patient.query: I guess it’s like I have this history of being disappointed, either by myself or by others. The times I’ve tried to be better or make changes just didn’t go the way I hoped. It’s hard to shake that feeling when I look back and see so many times I didn’t measure up. Sometimes it feels like everyone else is moving forward while I’m just... here.

Those moments just replay in my mind, and it makes me question if I’m ever going to really get it right. It’s kind of draining to carry that around.

It just loops back and makes everything feel darker.
